finding_results.notes: The closing turn now names the unresolved mismatch as unaccounted-for bedroom traffic around the 3:15 AM voicemail deletion and the pills removed from Martin's room, rather than leaving Mallick with an abstract hunch.
Removed both instances of 'the specific' and rewrote the repeated negation-correction beats so the only major retained case-note negation is Mallick's notebook line.
Kept the opening refrigerator-timing establishment and reduced the deck ambiance to a single loon call. The repeated refrigerator and loon callbacks were cut or replaced.
The chapter now contains two 'refrigerator' string matches, both in the opening establishment passage, which is below the threshold of three references.
Reduced em-dash usage to three dialogue-interruption lines. Verification after revision showed 3 em-dash lines across 108 prose lines, well below the 10% threshold.
Reduced 'something' usage to three instances and replaced the prior abstract hedging at the structural turn with named evidence.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:161; chapters/chapter_10.md:163; chapters/chapter_10.md:167; chapters/chapter_10.md:169; chapters/chapter_10.md:173; chapters/chapter_10.md:175; chapters/chapter_10.md:179; chapters/chapter_10.md:189
finding_results.notes: Replaced single thesis-speech block with evidentiary back-and-forth. Mallick now delivers Paul's specific accusation in two short beats (lines 163, 167). Paul makes the key inferential leap himself: he lists the three evidence items, connects them to three different rooms, and concludes 'Those were three different people' (line 173). Mallick's 'At least' (line 175) expands without lecturing. Paul volunteers hearing people at night (line 179), and Mallick finishes his trailing thought. The 'committee' label is preserved in Mallick's private narration (line 47) but no longer appears as a mic-drop in dialogue. Cut: 'because you loved your friend,' the full house-theory summary, 'A committee that never met' in dialogue, 'scared of the same thing' psychological summary, and 'sad and dumb and very human' moral framing. The bedroom-traffic gap still lands through concrete evidence (line 189).

Replaced 'her mother had raised her to believe you didn't show up empty-handed to a place where people were suffering' with 'that was what she did — somebody died, she cooked something, she brought it.' The new phrasing frames the casserole as Mallick's ingrained professional habit rather than maternal instruction. No mother reference, no 'show up empty-handed' construction. Preserves Mallick's domestic register and matter-of-fact voice. The subsequent reasons for the habit (the gesture, the cooking, the Pyrex-as-badge) remain unchanged.

Replaced the later slogan-style restatement with new investigative payload: Mallick now turns the multi-tamperer insight into a practical reading of how the lies will diverge and records a bedroom/hall traffic question in her notebook. After the Paul re-interview, the closing beat now captures what his answers actually prove and narrows the remaining witness-gap problem instead of repeating the same five-panics formulation. Also corrected the casserole timing to align with the Saturday death call while preserving the beat.

pass_summaries.summary: Rebuilt the chapter around a concrete unresolved bedroom-timeline gap, compressed repeated explanatory passes over the five-tamperer theory, and cleared the packet's formula, motif, em-dash, refrigerator, loon, and 'something' thresholds without changing the chapter's ending state.

Two dialogue findings addressed. The reveal scene (CH10_DIALOGUE_001) was reworked from a single diagnostic lecture into a pressure-and-response exchange where Paul supplies the central inference — connecting three blank answers to three different tamperers — and Mallick confirms and extends with concrete evidence. The casserole rationale (CH10_DIALOGUE_002) was rewritten to replace the style-bible-adjacent mother-plus-empty-handed construction with a habit-based phrasing native to Mallick's private register. Both fixes preserve the chapter's engine (Mallick realizes multiple independent tamperers), its state shift (bedroom traffic gap remains the open question), and all must-land beats. No structural, continuity, or downstream changes introduced.

Chapter 10's repeated committee/five-panics insight was compressed into sharper investigative consequence. The initial realization remains, while the later beats now advance Mallick's method and the unresolved bedroom traffic problem instead of re-certifying the same theory.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=9, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
